note: The blades and powers that rise to test Saehara's name, keyed to his prestige band (see the_name_that_travels.recurrence). Authored to be RICH and authentic — samurai tradition first, magic/fantasy woven in. Each is a duel opponent (SNG-098 skill battle) with a decline/flee path. The GM picks by band and may mint variations.

challengers.name: A wandering blade
challengers.concept: A dōjō-yaburi hopeful — young, hungry, testing an unproven name to make their own. Competent, not yet dangerous. The first rung.
challengers.style: straightforward kenjutsu; more nerve than mastery
challengers.duelStakes: prestige only — a loss here is a bruised pride, not a grave
challengers.voiceHints: Eager, a little too loud, trying on a swagger that doesn't quite fit yet. Bows correctly, at least.

challengers.name: Ito of the Still-Water School
challengers.concept: Champion of a local ryūha, sent to defend the lineage's honor against a masterless ronin whose very existence is an argument that schools don't matter. Disciplined, patient, fights for something larger than himself.
challengers.style: Still-Water kenjutsu — defensive, counter-timed, waits for the opponent to overcommit; zanshin held long after each exchange
challengers.duelStakes: the school's honor vs. the ronin's road — a philosophical duel as much as a physical one
challengers.voiceHints: Formal, measured, genuinely respectful even as he means to defeat you. Speaks of his school the way others speak of family.

challengers.name: Kaede the Unlineaged
challengers.concept: Another masterless blade — a mirror of Saehara walking the same pilgrimage. Not an enemy, exactly; a rival who understands the road because she walks it too. Recurs: she may lose, return, sharpen, become a sworn rival or a friend.
challengers.style: adaptive, self-taught, unpredictable — no school's tells because she has no school
challengers.duelStakes: two roads, one question — whose way of being masterless is truer
challengers.voiceHints: Dry, knowing, treats the duel as a conversation. The only one who calls Saehara by what he is rather than what he's becoming.

challengers.name: Ren of the Crimson Ledger
challengers.concept: A named duelist whose OWN prestige now demands he face Saehara — two rising names cannot both go unmeasured. He keeps a ledger of every duel he's fought; Saehara is the entry he's been waiting to write.
challengers.style: precise, record-keeping, studies his opponent's past duels before fighting — comes prepared with counters
challengers.duelStakes: two renowns collide — the valley will talk about this one for years
challengers.voiceHints: Cold courtesy, the manner of a man who has already imagined this duel a hundred times. Writes in his ledger before and after.

challengers.name: Suzu of the Ember Draw
challengers.concept: The fantasy layer made flesh — a sword-saint whose blade is fire. She proves steel is only one language: her draw ignites, her strikes carry heat, her iaijutsu is a discipline of flame. Magic and swordsmanship as one art.
challengers.style: iaijutsu fused with blazeborn fire-craft — the draw itself is an ignition; fast, aggressive, punishes hesitation
challengers.duelStakes: can a mind-guided blade (cogitant) read a fire that changes as it burns? steel vs. flame
challengers.voiceHints: Warm, fierce, almost joyful in the contest — she loves a worthy blade the way fire loves dry wood. No cruelty, all intensity.

challengers.name: The Blade Without a Name
challengers.concept: The Ganryūjima beat — the challenge that defines the arc. A duelist who may genuinely be Saehara's equal or better, and who (fittingly) has refused a name, as Saehara nearly is one. Who they truly are, and what the duel costs, the GM shapes from how Saehara has walked the whole arc: whom he spared, whether he killed for the name, whether he chose the brush over the blade.
challengers.style: a mirror and a culmination — the GM builds their style to answer Saehara's own; if he's been merciful, they test his mercy; if he's killed, they are the reckoning; if he's chosen the pen, they are the blade that asks if the pen was cowardice or wisdom
challengers.duelStakes: the title of the book — saint, killer, or teacher — is written here
challengers.voiceHints: Still. Utterly still. The stillness Saehara himself carries, reflected back and deepened. Says little; what they say, Saehara will remember for the rest of the story.
